name: Margherita Pizza
description: A classic Neapolitan pizza topped with San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, basil, and olive oil on a thin crust.
funFact: Named after Queen Margherita of Savoy who visited Naples in 1889.

name: Pad Thai
description: Stir-fried rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, eggs, bean sprouts, and tamarind sauce, topped with crushed peanuts and lime.
funFact: Pad Thai was promoted as a national dish during WWII when rice was scarce.

name: Tacos al Pastor
description: Marinated pork cooked on a vertical spit, served on corn tortillas with pineapple, onion, cilantro, and salsa.
funFact: Brought to Mexico by Lebanese immigrants in the early 1900s, inspired by shawarma.

name: Sushi
description: Vinegared rice paired with fresh raw fish, seafood, and vegetables, often wrapped in nori seaweed.
funFact: Sushi originated as a method of preserving fish in fermented rice in the 2nd century.

name: Butter Chicken
description: Tender chicken pieces in a rich, creamy tomato-based curry with butter, cream, and aromatic spices.
funFact: Invented by accident at Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s.

name: Peking Duck
description: Roasted duck with crispy skin, served with thin pancakes, spring onions, cucumber, and sweet hoisin sauce.
funFact: A royal dish since the Yuan Dynasty (1271–1368), with over 600 years of history.

name: Paella
description: Saffron-infused rice dish with seafood, chicken, rabbit, green beans, and bell peppers, cooked in a wide shallow pan.
funFact: Authentic paella is traditionally cooked over an open fire with orange branches for smoky flavor.

name: Shawarma
description: Seasoned meat slow-roasted on a vertical spit, thinly sliced and served in pita with tahini, pickles, and vegetables.
funFact: The word 'shawarma' comes from the Turkish word 'çevirme', meaning 'turning'.

name: Bibimbap
description: A bowl of warm rice topped with sautéed vegetables, gochujang chili paste, sliced meat, and a fried egg.
funFact: Originally a meal for farmers during harvest season, mixing leftovers into one bowl.

name: Espresso
description: A concentrated coffee brewed by forcing hot water through finely-ground beans under high pressure, served in a small demitasse cup.
funFact: The first espresso machine was invented in Turin in 1884 — patented as a 'steam beverage machine'.

name: Masala Chai
description: Black tea simmered with milk, sugar, and a fragrant blend of spices including cardamom, cinnamon, ginger, and cloves.
funFact: Chai wallahs (tea vendors) serve over 1 billion cups of chai daily across India.

name: Mojito
description: A refreshing cocktail of white rum, fresh lime juice, mint leaves, sugar, and soda water, served over crushed ice.
funFact: Ernest Hemingway was a famous fan of the mojito at La Bodeguita del Medio bar in Havana.

name: Sake
description: A traditional rice wine brewed through fermentation of polished rice, water, koji mold, and yeast — served warm or chilled.
funFact: Sake brewing dates back over 2,000 years and is often called 'nihonshu' — the drink of the gods.

name: Moroccan Mint Tea
description: A sweet blend of Chinese green tea, fresh spearmint, and sugar, ceremonially poured from a height into small ornate glasses.
funFact: In Morocco, refusing a glass of mint tea is considered impolite — it's the essential gesture of hospitality.
